threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to stand on the right on the escalators. Hope you enjoy your morning workout dodging the locals!
Last night on the 77, a saxophonist jumped on at Brixton and played until he got off at Stockwell. That's the kind of late-night magic you don't get on the tube.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy juggling flaming torches at Hermannplatz while his dog waited patiently next to a kebab shop.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a currywurst at 4 AM after a night out, while street artists create spontaneous graffiti performances on the sidewalks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always tell who's local by whether they know the secret entrance to Dolores Park. Tourists just get lost at the main gate.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo serenade a lady with a ukulele on the 14-Mission. Only in SF do you catch a romantic moment like that on your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas and the local bakery smell that hits you at dawn.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their confusion at the lack of signs for 'caution' at crossings—watching them hesitate at every corner is like a comedy show.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when buses don’t stop at every stop like they do in other cities. Trust me, they skip a few out here.
Caught the night bus last week; a guy serenaded the driver with a ukulele while a cat tried to sneak in through the back door. Classic Peckham vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they complain about the BART delays while juggling a burrito, not just scrolling Twitter for updates.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling while riding the 14 Mission. One hand on the pole, the other tossing oranges. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they order a 'café con leche'—if they don't leave space for the foam, they’re fresh off the plane.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a toddler sharing a Berliner Weisse with their Opa at the café on Weserstraße—now that's Neukölln living.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln will you find a bar serving cocktails in old shampoo bottles. It's bizarre and oddly charming. A true local touch.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'tapas' is just a snack. In Gràcia, it’s a full-on meal—locals go for the whole experience, not just a plate or two.
